name: Orion
description: One of the most recognisable constellations, representing a hunter in Greek mythology.

name: Lyra
description: A small constellation containing Vega, one of the vertices of the Summer Triangle.

name: Aquila
description: The eagle constellation, home to Altair, another vertex of the Summer Triangle.

name: Cygnus
description: The swan constellation; Deneb forms the tail of the swan and the Summer Triangle's third vertex.

name: Canis Major
description: The 'greater dog' constellation containing Sirius, the brightest star in the night sky.

name: Taurus
description: The bull constellation, one of the oldest recognised constellations.

name: Leo
description: The lion constellation, one of the zodiacal constellations.

name: Scorpius
description: The scorpion constellation; Antares marks the scorpion's heart.

name: Crux
description: The smallest of the 88 modern constellations but one of the most distinctive in the southern sky.

name: Gemini
description: The twins constellation; Pollux and Castor mark the heads of the twins.
